title: OpenAI details how population-level crash analysis found an 18-year-old infrastructure bug
friendlyTitle: OpenAI explains how it fixed rare infrastructure crashes
summary: OpenAI engineering described how fleet-wide core-dump analysis separated two rare Rockset crash causes: a faulty Azure host and an old GNU libunwind race condition affecting C++ exception unwinding.
friendlySummary: OpenAI found two hidden causes behind strange crashes in systems used for ChatGPT-related data services.
whyItMatters: Reliable AI products depend on unglamorous infrastructure work. For engineering teams, the lesson is concrete: broad crash classification and high-quality telemetry can solve problems that single-incident debugging cannot.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Good AI tools need reliable plumbing. Better monitoring and debugging make AI products more dependable for everyone.
